source.page_label: WA Migratory Game Bird Seasons | eRegulations
source.update_note: 2026-27 migratory bird data from WDFW regulations. Washington is in the Pacific Flyway with complex goose management areas.

species.name: Duck & Coot
species.season_raw: Oct 11-19 & Oct 22 - Jan 25 (split season). Youth days: Sep 20 (west), Sep 27 (east).

species.name: Goose (Canada & White-fronted)
species.season_raw: Early Canada: Sep 6-14 (GMAs 1-3). Regular: Oct 11 - Jan 25 (varies by GMA).
species.bag_limit: 3 Canada daily (GMA 1&3), 2 Canada (GMA 2); 10 white-fronted; 6-10 white geese

licenses.name: Small Game License (Resident)
licenses.covers: Required for all hunting including migratory birds

licenses.name: Migratory Bird Permit
licenses.covers: Required for all migratory bird hunting (includes HIP)

key_notes.title: The Washington Migratory Bird Permit ($23.27) is required for all migratory bird hunting in addition to the Small Game License. Includes HIP registration.

sources.label: WDFW — License Fees
sources.evidence: Migratory Bird Permit: $23.27. Required for all migratory bird hunting.

key_notes.title: Washington has a 7-duck daily bag limit (higher than the standard 6 in most states). Species restrictions apply: 2 hen mallards, 3 pintail, 2 scaup, 2 canvasback, 2 redhead.

sources.label: WDFW — Duck Limits
sources.evidence: 7 ducks daily. Max 2 hen mallard, 3 pintail, 2 scaup, 2 canvasback, 2 redhead.

key_notes.title: Washington has 3 Goose Management Areas (GMAs) with different Canada goose bag limits and season dates. Dusky Canada goose is closed.

sources.label: WDFW — Goose Management Areas
sources.evidence: GMA 1&3: 3 Canada daily. GMA 2: 2 Canada daily. Dusky Canada goose closed.

key_notes.title: Band-tailed pigeon has a very short season (Sep 15-23) with a 2-bird daily bag limit. Hunters must report band-tailed pigeon harvest.

sources.label: WDFW — Band-tailed Pigeon
sources.evidence: Band-tailed Pigeon: Sep 15-23. 2 daily, 6 possession. Reporting required.
